sources.name: Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level Formula
sources.note: Syllables per word is the secondary FK driver. Formula coefficient: 11.8 × (syllables/words). Higher syllable-per-word ratio raises FK grade.

sources.name: COCA Frequency Data
sources.note: High-frequency words in COCA are predominantly 1-2 syllables. Words above rank 5000 trend toward 3+ syllables.

sources.name: BNC/COCA Word Family Lists 1k-2k bands
sources.note: 1k-2k bands are dominated by 1-2 syllable words. Low-syllable high-frequency baseline for plain language composition.

sources.name: Plain Language Act 2010
sources.note: Prefer short words. Use technical terms only when necessary.

sources.name: Max Planck Institute — AI Linguistic Imprinting Study 2024
sources.note: Words like delve, robust, and pivotal spiked over 50% in published text after ChatGPT release. Documented AI signature vocabulary.

sources.name: PubMed / medRxiv — Delving into PubMed Records 2024
sources.note: Corpus study of AI word frequency spikes in academic literature. Words ranked by anomalous rate post-2022: delve, underscore, meticulous, commendable, showcase, intricate, tapestry, realm, cutting-edge, prowess.

sources.name: Embryo — Cross-referenced AI Overuse Word List (March 2025)
sources.note: Cross-referenced list from Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, Qwen, DeepSeek output analysis. Validated against GPTZero AI vocabulary checker.

sources.name: Wikipedia — Signs of AI Writing
sources.note: Field guide of AI writing patterns documented in Wikipedia articles. GPT-4 and GPT-4o era word lists documented with real examples.

_description: Syllables per word is the secondary FK driver. Prefer 1-2 syllable words. Three or more polysyllabic words in sequence triggers a rewrite. AI signature words are always blocked regardless of domain or syllable count — they carry no meaning that a simpler word cannot carry, and they signal machine-generated text.
_critical_rule: Syllable load is checked at step 6 before grammar and syntax rules apply. AI signature words are blocked at this step — not at the register layer. A word may pass the register ceiling check and still be blocked here if it appears on the AI signature list.

targets.permitted_with_justification: 3 syllables — when no 1-2 syllable word carries the same precise meaning
targets.domain_exception_only: 4+ syllables — only when domain_exceptions.json permits and domain tag matches
targets.sequence_trigger: 3 or more consecutive words of 3+ syllables triggers a rewrite of that phrase

syllable_rules.rule: Prefer 1-2 syllable words for all vocabulary selections.
syllable_rules.rationale: 1-2 syllable words are the most frequent in English across all corpora. They are processed faster, retained more easily, and understood by the widest audience.
syllable_rules.example_correct: The road is blocked. Use the south exit.
syllable_rules.example_incorrect: The thoroughfare is obstructed. Utilize the southerly egress.

syllable_rules.rule: Use a 3-syllable word only when no 1-2 syllable word carries the same precise meaning.
syllable_rules.rationale: Three-syllable words are not blocked. The constraint is that a simpler word must be attempted first.
syllable_rules.example_correct_3syl: The committee will analyze the data.
syllable_rules.example_incorrect_3syl: The committee will carry out an analysis of the data.
syllable_rules.source: vocabulary selection order in pipeline.json step 5

syllable_rules.rule: Words of 4+ syllables require a domain exception entry.
syllable_rules.rationale: Words of 4+ syllables are rarely in everyday use. Without a domain exception, they must be replaced.
syllable_rules.action: Check domain_exceptions.json. If no entry exists for the domain, replace with the nearest 1-2 syllable equivalent.

syllable_rules.rule: Three or more consecutive words of 3+ syllables triggers a rewrite of that phrase.
syllable_rules.rationale: A sequence of polysyllabic words forces the reader to decode multiple complex words with no cognitive rest.
syllable_rules.example_incorrect: The organizational assessment committee evaluated the proposal.
syllable_rules.action: Identify the word in the sequence with the best 1-2 syllable replacement. Replace it to break the sequence.

syllable_rules.rule: Do not replace a precise term with a shorter word that loses meaning.
syllable_rules.rationale: Syllable reduction is secondary to meaning preservation.
syllable_rules.source: conflict_resolution.json — vocabulary precision overrides syllable optimization

syllable_rules.rule: Proper nouns and active domain exceptions are exempt from syllable rules.
syllable_rules.rationale: A name or domain term of art cannot be replaced with a shorter word.

ai_signature_blocklist._description: Words and phrases that appear at anomalously high rates in LLM output relative to human writing corpora. Grounded in the Max Planck Institute study, PubMed corpus analysis, Embryo cross-referenced list, and Wikipedia AI writing signs documentation. All entries are blocked regardless of domain, register ceiling, or FK target. No exceptions.
ai_signature_blocklist._action: Delete or replace with the direct plain English equivalent. If no equivalent exists because the word carries no real meaning, delete it entirely.
_sources: Max Planck Institute 2024 study — 50%+ spike in AI text post-ChatGPT
PubMed medRxiv corpus study 2024 — ranked anomalous rate list
Embryo cross-referenced list March 2025 — GPTZero validated
Wikipedia Signs of AI Writing — GPT-4 and GPT-4o era documented patterns

verbs.note: Anomalously high LLM rate. Rare in human writing. Top of PubMed ranked list.
Phrase variant of delve. Same block.
No meaning beyond use. Always replace.
Business jargon. No added meaning over use.
Nominalization trap. Replace with direct verb.
Vague. Replace with what is actually happening.
Overused in LLM motivational and professional output.

Vague. Name what is actually being given or allowed.

Business filler. Name the specific change.

Overused in LLM narrative and motivational output.
Vague emotional filler.
Marketing filler.
Overused in LLM aspirational language.
Vague. Name the specific change.
Unnecessary complexity. Replace with direct verb.
Overused in AI tech and business writing.
Marketing filler. Name what is opened or accessed.
Marketing filler. No added meaning.
Overused. Change is almost always sufficient.
Vague. Name the specific mechanism.
Unnecessarily formal. Replace with direct verb.

adjectives.note: Top AI signature word. Max Planck 50%+ spike.
Replace with the specific claim it makes.
No precise meaning. Replace with what is included.
Used as filler. Describe the nuance — do not label it.
Max Planck 50%+ spike. PubMed ranked list.
Anomalously high in PubMed study.

PubMed ranked list.

Claim it specifically instead of labelling it.

Marketing filler. Name the specific capability.

Wikipedia AI writing signs list.
Overused formal filler.
Replace with the specific quantity or scale.
Vague. List the facets instead.

Vague intensifier.
Overused in AI output.

nouns.note: PubMed ranked list. Replace with the specific domain.
Academic filler. Usually replaceable with a simpler word.
Business jargon. Name the specific benefit.
Always replace with the specific group: staff, residents, contractors.
Tech filler. Name the specific system.
Metaphorical filler. Name the actual process.
Overused in analytical and business AI output.

phrases.note: Filler. The point is either important or it is not.
The conclusion is the last paragraph. No label needed.

phrases.replace_with: delete or rewrite as but / however

phrases.phrase: at the end of the day

phrases.note: Phrase variant. Same block as delve.

phrases.replace_with: delete — start with the actual point

phrases.note: Unnecessarily formal. Many is always sufficient.

phrases.replace_with: delete or rewrite — use and or but
phrases.note: Transitional filler. If the logic is clear, no connector is needed.

common_replacements.description: High-frequency polysyllabic words and their plain language replacements — syllable reduction only, not AI signature
